Murielle, I love a good career story. How long have you worked in HR?
I have worked in HR for fifteen years. My previous role was in a small consultancy.
Nice. What has been your biggest milestone?
While I was leading a recruitment project, we launched a new program and hired forty people.
Impressive. Were you working there during the merger?
Yes. While the company was going through the merger, I spearheaded the restructuring.
That sounds hard. Have you ever regretted it?
No, I have not. It was tough, but I have never forgotten what it taught me.
I have worked in HR for fifteen years.
While I was leading the merger, I spearheaded the downsizing.
Last year we completed a real turnaround.
I have led three major restructurings.
Looking back, every milestone has taught me something.

Hi Murielle, it is Liam again. You asked about my career, so here is the longer version. I have worked in HR for almost fifteen years now, and it has been quite a journey. I started at a small recruitment agency in Sydney, and while I was learning the basics, my manager gave me my first real project. A few years later, I moved to a big mining company, and that was a genuine milestone for me. I spearheaded a new onboarding program for the whole region, and I have never forgotten how proud I felt. In 2018, while the company was going through a merger, I led the difficult restructuring. It was not easy, but I have learned more from that one year than from any other. We should compare stories one day. Talk soon.

Good afternoon, everyone. I want to share a story from last year, because it has become one of my proudest achievements. When the downsizing began, people were feeling anxious, and morale was falling fast. So while the leadership team was still debating the numbers, we designed a clear communication plan and held weekly meetings with every department. We did not promise easy answers, but we listened, and we kept people informed. Slowly, the mood changed. By the end of the year, we had completed a real turnaround: we kept most of our key people, and the turnover stayed low. I have led several difficult projects in my career, but that one taught me the most about trust. Thank you all.
